children.heading: Additional protocol
children.label: 1 Additional protocol
children.text: The term “Additional Protocol”, when used in the singular form, means the Protocol Additional to the Agreement between the United States of America and the International Atomic Energy Agency for the Application of Safeguards in the United States of America, with Annexes, signed at Vienna (T. Doc. 107–7).June 12, 1998

children.heading: Appropriate congressional committees
children.label: 2 Appropriate congressional committees
children.text: The term “appropriate congressional committees” means the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Foreign Relations, and the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate and the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on International Relations, the Committee on Science, and the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Complementary access
children.label: 3 Complementary access
children.text: The term “complementary access” means the exercise of the IAEA’s access rights as set forth in Articles 4 to 6 of the Additional Protocol.

children.heading: Executive agency
children.label: 4 Executive agency
children.text: The term “executive agency” has the meaning given such term in .section 105 of title 5

children.heading: Facility
children.label: 5 Facility
children.text: The term “facility” has the meaning set forth in Article 18i. of the Additional Protocol.

children.heading: IAEA
children.label: 6 IAEA
children.text: The term “IAEA” means the International Atomic Energy Agency.

children.heading: Judge of the United States
children.label: 7 Judge of the United States
children.text: The term “judge of the United States” means a United States district judge, or a United States magistrate judge appointed under the authority of chapter 43 of title 28.

children.heading: Location
children.label: 8 Location
children.text: The term “location” means any geographic point or area declared or identified by the United States or specified by the International Atomic Energy Agency.

children.heading: Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty
children.label: 9 Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty
children.text: The term “Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty” means the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons, done at Washington, London, and Moscow , and entered into force (21 UST 483).July 1, 1968March 5, 1970

children.heading: Nuclear-weapon State Party and non-nuclear-weapon State Party
children.label: 10 Nuclear-weapon State Party and non-nuclear-weapon State Party
children.text: The terms “nuclear-weapon State Party” and “non-nuclear-weapon State Party” have the meanings given such terms in the Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty.

children.heading: Person
children.label: 11 Person
children.text: The term “person”, except as otherwise provided, means any individual, corporation, partnership, firm, association, trust, estate, public or private institution, any State or any political subdivision thereof, or any political entity within a State, any foreign government or nation or any agency, instrumentality, or political subdivision of any such government or nation, or other entity located in the United States.

children.heading: Site
children.label: 12 Site
children.text: The term “site” has the meaning set forth in Article 18b. of the Additional Protocol.

children.label: A
children.text: the territorial sea and the overlying airspace;

children.label: B
children.text: any civil aircraft of the United States or public aircraft, as such terms are defined in paragraphs (17) and (41), respectively, of ; andsection 40102(a) of title 49

children.label: C
children.text: any vessel of the United States, as such term is defined in .section 70502(b) of title 46

children.heading: Wide-area environmental sampling
children.label: 14 Wide-area environmental sampling
children.text: The term “wide-area environmental sampling” has the meaning set forth in Article 18g. of the Additional Protocol.
